description: Explicit exceptions to the list-page pagination rule (Req 12.3). Each entry documents WHY a list is safe to render unpaginated — typically because the result set is bounded by user-scoped RLS (one student, one course, one teacher) and cannot grow to institution scale. Before adding an exception, confirm the query's WHERE clause genuinely bounds the row count.

fileLevel.rationale: Student's own journal entries — bounded by student_id via RLS. A single student's journal realistically contains <200 entries over a semester. If any student exceeds 500 entries we'll revisit with .limit(200) + infinite scroll.

Student's own active + upcoming + completed challenges — bounded by student_id. Challenge count per student typically <30 across all statuses. Revisit if platform adds long-running streak-based challenges.

Student's own assignments filtered by course — bounded by course_id + student enrollment. A single course usually has <50 assignments per semester. Revisit if courses with >100 assignments ship (e.g., daily quiz courses).
Teacher's challenges filtered by courseId — bounded by course. Typically <20 per course per semester. Revisit if bulk challenge import ships (EDU-2400).
